entry.title: Powerful way to create a CDS view based report on BOMs in SAP S4 HANA Cloud (Public Edition)
entry.content:
Since, most of our reporting requirements in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud are met through CDS Views, I’m herewith sharing my experience on how end user life can be made easy, through usage of right CDS views, and converting the same into an easily consumable app!

Here we are creating a BOM report using custom CDS view and analytical query which will display the details of all existing Bill of materials for a range of materials within plant.

Check this out –

Step 1. Create custom CDS views to fetch data in custom analytical query.

Step 2. Create a custom analytical query.

Step 3. Create application (Fiori tile) for query and assign it to Business catalog.

Selection Screen:

Final Output of Report:

Steps 1. Create custom CDS views.

Within the SAP Fiori environment, open the app 'Custom CDS Views' which can be found in the category Extensibility.

Click on Create.

Fill Label (Name of CDS) and select scenario as ‘Standard CDS View’ and click on create.

*We are using the scenario as ‘standard CDS view’ because we will use this CDS in another custom CDS view using joint conditions.

Select Primary data source as ‘I_BillOfMaterialItemDEX_2’

Click on Add to an associate data source.

Click on Associate data source.

Select associate data source as ‘I_Product’

Click on Joint Condition

Click on Add

Select Product

Select    Value type = Field

              Value = I_BillOfMaterialItemDEX_2.BillOfMaterialComponent

              Cardinality = Zero or One

Go to Element Tab: Click on Add – Elements

Add below mentioned elements.

Click on Check.

Once Check is successfully done, Click on Publish

Custom CDS Published Successfully.

Now we will create another CDS of scenario ‘Analytical cube’ which we will use in Analytical query.

Go to App: Custom CDS Views.

Click on Create

Fill Label and select scenario as ‘Analytical Cube.’

Select Primary data source as ‘I_MaterialBOMLink’

Click on Add an Associate data source.

Select CDS view which we have created earlier.

Click on Joint Condition

Add Joint condition- BillOfMaterial and BillOfMaterialVariant

Select value type as Field and value as BillOfMaterial & BillOfMaterialVariant

Cardinality= Zero and One

Go To Elementsà Click on Add Elements

Add below mentioned elements.

Go to Element Properties à Select ‘No Operation’ for ‘BillOfMaterialItemQuantity’

Click on Check

Click on Publish

Step 2. Create a custom analytical query.

Go to App: Custom Analytical Queries

Click on Create

Mention Query Name and Select Data source as CDS created in Step 1.

In General Tab: Give Label (Name of Query Report)

Go to Field Selection view and select required fields for report

Use Up Down Option in Display Tab to set sequence for fields in report.

Use Override label option to change the column name.

Go to Filter Option

Click on Field à Select User Unit Values

Selection type = Interval, select the multiple selection checkbox.

Click on Save Draft Option

Mention Plant and click on Go.

Report Displayed.

Come back to query screen and click on Publish.

Step 3. Create application (Fiori tile) for query and assign it to Business catalog.

Go to App: View browser – Browse Views

Search your custom analytical query, select, and click on create application.

 

Give Report title and subtitle and click on ok.

Application ID will generate for the query.

 

Click on Application ID and Go to ‘view in catalog’ option

 

Click on Add to add this application to business catalog.

Select Business catalog.

*You can assign this application to multiple business catalog.

Select the catalog and click on Publish.

App Published successfully for selected business catalog.

Click on Business catalog.

Go to More and select used in Business roles.

Now assign one of the business roles to your user ID.

Go to SAP Home screen.

Search App: BOM Report

Fill the selection parameter and click on Go.

 

Thanks & Regards,

Manish Sati

entry.title: Document Management System (DMS) Integration with Production in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Welcome to our comprehensive guide on integrating DMS documents with production orders in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. In this blog, we'll walk you through the setup process, demonstrating how production operators and supervisors can easily access engineering drawings and material designs linked to the production order. This seamless integration is crucial for maintaining accuracy and efficiency on the shop floor.

Introduction:

This blog outlines the process for integrating a DMS document linked to the material master with the production order in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. It also explains how production operators or supervisors can view the automatically linked DMS document for engineering drawings or material design within the production order. This integration helps shop floor operators keep track of each document in the production order, ensuring the correct production process is followed in shop floor control.

Set Up for Integrating DMS Documents with Production Order:

  • Define Production Scheduling Profile – Tick Mark the ‘Document Links – Material’ Checkbox.

  • Assign the Production Scheduling Profile in Material Master using MM02 (Change Material Master) app.

  • Upload Engineering/Design Document (Document name can be in preferred language) with type SPP (Simplified PP) in the Material Master using Fiori app ‘Manage Product Master Data’. Upon Saving, it generates a DMS Document Number of type SPP (Simplified PP). Click on that link to view the DMS Document generated.

  • Goto CV02n (Change Document), Input the DMS Document Number (Document type SPP) noted in previous step and add the Description in the preferred language.

  • View the Original Document attached under the Originals Tab

  • View the Material number linked under the Object Links tab.

DMS Integration with Production Order:

  • Create Production Order

  • Click on ‘Documents’ Button to view the DMS Document linked.

  • At the time of order creation, DMS Document link attached automatically as per the setup made in Production scheduling profile.

  • In the Main Header Screen, Click on Tree View Button.

  • Right click on the Document Id > Click on Documents > Click on Document Name.

  • Original document that is uploaded in material master displayed in a new tab in the browser.

  • Production Order Saved.

  • Uploaded document can also be viewed in CO02 (Change Production Order) and CO03 (Display Production Order) screen in the same way by clicking Tree View.
  • Production Order number will be assigned under Object Links tab in the DMS Document (CV03n – Display Document).

By following the steps outlined in this blog, you can ensure that all relevant documents are readily available to your shop floor operators, streamlining the production process and enhancing overall control. Stay tuned for more tips and insights on optimizing your production workflows and maintaining high standards of quality and efficiency in your operations.

Hope this is useful and for any further queries, please post your queries in the SAP Community Portal

Happy Learning!!

entry.title: Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
Using easy-to-use tools and interfaces to create and control intelligent scenarios can assist in tackling difficulties associated with complexity and data accuracy. This approach can lead to improved understanding and suggestions, especially with the new Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Completion, resulting in higher customer satisfaction and business growth. Learn more about how the innovative Auto-Activation of Intelligent Scenario enhances automation in my most recent blog post.

Additionally, I will walk you through my selected key highlights for Sales:

To name just a few highlights:

  • Auto-Activation of Intelligent Scenario for Input Recommendations for Sales Orders,
  • New SAP Fiori app to Display the Change Log for Sales Documents,
  • Improvement of Flexible Default Item Category Determination in Sales Documents,
  • Enhanced SAP Fiori app “Manage Customer Returns – Version 2”,
  • … and more.

In case you do not need all the information below and just briefly want to get an overview of the release highlights, please watch this video with the top three key innovations:

Release video: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 for Sales: Top 3 key innovations.

 

Auto-Activation of Intelligent Scenario for Input Recommendations for Sales Orders

Business Background

Sales teams receive many sales orders from various channels and APIs daily. Unfortunately, some of these orders are missing important information, making them unable to be handled further. Fixing these errors requires a significant amount of manual work in data management.

With the last release, we delivered the first feature concerning the Sales Order Auto-Completion and it’s about Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Completion. Over the use of historical data and machine learning, the system can now offer proposals for filling in empty fields in an incomplete sales order.

Implementing intelligent scenarios often requires a deep understanding of prediction models, data analytics, and model training techniques. With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release, we introduce the Auto-Activation of Intelligent Scenario feature via Turnkey AI, aligning with the broader goal of leveraging technology to drive digital transformation and improve business performance.

Key Feature

To enable the Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Completion functionality and display recommendations in the sales orders user interface, customers typically have to undertake several manual setup procedures.

Below you find some challenges on the customer side:

  • Create the connection between SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and the SAP BTP Platform or train the model on historical sales order data.
  • The activation of a Machine-Learning (ML) scenario is time-consuming and requires many manual steps,
  • Data Science knowledge is required to enable the ML scenarios,
  • Process knowledge is required.

 Picture 1: Automatic set-up, training, and activation of intelligent scenario for input recommendations for sales order completion.

Turnkey AI addresses these challenges by enabling SAP S4/HANA ML scenarios to be activated automatically without user intervention.

The turnkey feature enables you to automate all ML operations of an intelligent scenario. This means, that once turnkey is enabled for an intelligent scenario, all the operations, such as pre-requisites check, train, deployment, and activation are run automatically. By automating the ML operations, the turnkey feature significantly reduces manual effort during the initial activation of an SAP S/4HANA ML scenario and throughout its lifecycle management. You can schedule turnkey and monitor the schedule in the Turnkey tab. Further details about the schedule and runs are provided in the Turnkey Schedule and Turnkey Runs tabs.

 Demo 1: Fast activation of intelligent scenario for sales order recommendations.

Remark 1:

The turnkey option is available in the “Intelligent Scenario Management” app only. You can select the intelligent scenario that supports the turnkey and switch it on. Once switched on, you can continue with the predefined schedule for turnkey. Otherwise, modify the schedule details and create a new turnkey schedule as needed.

Remark 2:

Turnkey AI for Sales Order Auto-Completion is automatically enabled by activating the scope item (73P).

For more information, see Turnkey and Monitor Recommendations for Sales Document Completion.

 

New SAP Fiori app to Display the Change Log for Sales Documents

Business Background

Many industries have strict regulatory requirements that necessitate tracking and documenting changes made to sales documents for auditing and compliance purposes. An application that displays a log of changes helps to ensure compliance with these regulations.

In the same way, in case of disputes or legal issues, a detailed change log can serve as evidence to support or defend business decisions related to sales documents. It offers legal protection and clarity in such situations.

Key Features

With this release, we introduce a new SAP Fiori app “Display Change Logs - Sales Documents”, in which you as a sales manager or an internal sales representative can display change logs for sales documents.

In this app, a change log contains changes to a sales document. Each log entry stores information about a change to the document header, a document item, or a schedule line, and the change is logged on the field or table level:

  • The system logs a change on the table level when an entry is created in or deleted from a table. For example, when a sales order item is created, the system logs this change on the table level because a new entry is generated in the item table.
  • The system logs a change on the field level when the value of a field is altered (for example, when order reason "001" is changed to "002" or removed). You can view the old and new values of the field in this app.

Change Type

The changes to sales documents can be classified into three types:

  • Insert: A table entry is created (for example, a sales order item created in the item table).
  • Delete: A table entry is deleted (for example, a sales order item is deleted from the item table).
  • Change: A field value is altered (for example, the order reason field is altered).

 Demo 2: Enhanced Transparency by tracking all modifications made to sales documents, promoting transparency in the sales process.

In summary, the key features are:

  • Display changes to multiple types of sales documents, including sales orders, sales contracts, sales quotations, credit memo requests, and return orders in one list
  • Search changes using flexible criteria such as changed by changed on, sales document type, or the field that was changed.

For more information, see Display Change Logs - Sales Documents.

 

Improvement of Flexible Default Item Category Determination in Sales Documents

Business Background

An Item Category identifies different types of items (for example, free-of-charge items and text items) and determines how the system processes the item. Item Category is determined by SSCUI Assign Item Category.

By enabling flexible item category determination, businesses gain increased control over how item categories are assigned. This feature allows for a more accurate mapping of items to relevant categories based on factors like material type, sales organization, or distribution channel. For instance, the system can be configured to suggest the CBAO item category for sales order items when specific business criteria are fulfilled, leading to the automatic activation of the billing plan.

Key Features

With the last 2402 release, we have introduced the possibility, of creating business rules in the Business Rule Framework plus (BRFplus) so that the system determines the item categories for items in sales quotations, orders, and orders without charges.

Instead of proposing the item category configured in the configuration activity Assign Item Categories, the system can now, by default, propose an item category according to the business rules that you specified in the Business Rule Framework plus.

 Demo 3: Provide more flexibility to define business rules to determine the default item category.

Now with this new 2408 release, we made improvements in business rules in Business Rule Framework plus (BRFplus) to determine the default item category with the following additional fields:

  • Custom fields on the sales document header,
  • Plant,
  • Payment terms.

Effects on Configuration

As a key user for configuration, you create and assign BRFplus applications, functions, and expressions in the configuration activity Create BRFplus Objects for Sales Document Categories. After that, you navigate to the “Simplified BRFplus Workbench” app to define your business rules.

For more information, see Flexible Item Category Determination.

 

Enhanced SAP Fiori app “Manage Customer Returns – Version 2”

Business Background

A customer return is a process that involves the return of goods from a customer to a company location. With customer returns processing, you can handle all returns scenarios, such as returns made at the counter, returns that involve a shipment to the warehouse (including the subsequent reverse logistics of the goods), and direct shipments from a customer to a supplier.

Key Features

With this 2408 release, we made enhancements to the “Manage Customer Returns - Version 2” app, you can now do the following:

  • Create individual returns order items with a reference,
  • Select a different item category from the alternative options defined for the returns order,
  • Track the returns processing for a returns order item in the returns overview.

Create individual returns order items with a reference:

At the returns order item level, you can create single items concerning a sales order, an invoice, or a sales order without charge. The system then copies the relevant data from the reference document item to your returns order item. You can change the copied data and add additional information. For the same returns order, you can create items that reference different document categories. For example, in a returns order, you can create one item concerning a sales order and another concerning an invoice.

Select a different item category from the alternative options defined for the returns order:

Item category classifies different types of items, such as standard items and free-of-charge items. It determines how the system processes the item. When you enter a product in a returns order, the system automatically determines an item category. If alternative item categories are defined for the same product, you can now select a different item category from the alternative options for the returns order. Previously, the item category for the returns order item was read-only.

Track the returns processing for a returns order item in the returns overview:

With the returns overview, you can have an overview of all the steps required to complete the customer return process for a returns order item. By checking the processing status of each step, you can see whether a step is open, in process, complete, or contains errors. You can resolve the errors by navigating to the respective document in the step. You can monitor whether the subsequent documents related to the customer return have been successfully generated.

 Demo 4: Empower the returns and refund clerk with more functionalities in return order processing.

For more information, see Customer Returns Processing.

 

Creating External Billing Document Requests (EBDR) with Custom Fields by Importing Billing Data in Spreadsheet Format

Business Background

In previous releases, billing clerks could utilize the Create function within the “Manage Billing Document Requests” app to generate EBDRs by importing billing data from Microsoft® Excel® files. However, the importing of values for custom header and item fields added to EBDRs through the extensibility business contexts Sales: Billing Document and Sales: Billing Document Item was not supported.

Key Features

With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 update, any custom field that has been activated for the mentioned business contexts (and is consequently part of all EBDRs at the header or item level) can now be populated through the import of billing data from Microsoft® Excel® files when creating EBDRs.

This functionality is achieved by incorporating the custom fields as extra columns in the template file being imported.

 Picture 2: Entry of custom field data and other billing data into the Microsoft® Excel® template file.

For more information, see Creating External Billing Document Requests via Spreadsheet Import.

 

SAP Fiori app “Manage Debit Memo Requests - Version 2”: Item Creation with Reference, Preview of Billing Documents

Business Background

A Debit Memo Request in sales process management is a document used to request a credit or refund from a customer. It is typically issued by a seller to notify the buyer that a credit will be applied to their account, or a refund will be issued due to an overcharge, damaged goods, returned items, or any other situation where the buyer is owed money.

Key Features

The “Manage Debit Memo Requests - Version 2” app has been enhanced with the following features:

  • You can now create invoice increase requests (document type CBII) concerning an invoice. An invoice increase request can be used when the amount on the invoice needs to be increased. An invoice increase request is a type of debit memo request, but in contrast to standard debit memo requests, you cannot create an invoice increase request concerning a sales order or without a reference. You can only create an invoice increase request concerning an invoice or without reference. For more information about invoice increase requests, see Invoice Increase Request: Definition.
  • You can now display a preview of the billing document for the debit memo requests in this app. To preview the billing document, select a debit memo request in the list, and choose Preview Billing Document. The preview is displayed in a new window.
  • You can now add debit memo request items by referencing a sales order or an invoice. You can do so by selecting the Create with Reference button above the item table. For more information, see the Items Table.
  • You can now create, change, or display manual debit memo requests (document type DMR4) in this app. You can create them concerning a billing document, or without reference. Manual debit memo requests can only be used within the project-based services scenario.

 Picture 3: Empower internal sales representatives through increased functionalities in debit memo request processing.

For more information, see Manage Debit Memo Requests - Version 2.

 

SAP Fiori app “Manage Credit Memo Requests - Version 2”: Item Creation with Reference, Preview of Billing Documents

Business Background

A Credit Memo Request is a document used in sales process management to ask for a credit or adjustment to a customer's account. It is usually given by a seller to show that a credit will be given to the customer's account because of reasons like returned items, differences in pricing, or any other situation where the customer is owed a credit.

Key Features

The “Manage Credit Memo Requests - Version 2” app has been enhanced with the following features:

  • You can now display a preview of the billing document for the credit memo requests in this app. To preview the billing document, select a credit memo request from the list, and choose Preview Billing Document. The preview is displayed in a new window.
  • You can now add credit memo request items by referencing a sales order or an invoice. You can do so by selecting the Create with Reference button above the item table. For more information, see the Items Table.
  • You can now create, change, or display manual credit memo requests (document type CMR4) in this app. You can create them with reference to a billing document, or without reference. Manual credit memo requests can only be used within the project-based services scenario.

 Picture 4: Empower the internal sales representative with more functionalities in credit memo request processing.

For more information, see Manage Credit Memo Requests - Version 2.

 

Integration

Business Background

Integrating SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with external systems via new APIs based on RAP and OData V4 offers compelling advantages for modern applications:

  • RAP (Rapid Application Development): Simplifies development, allowing faster implementation of business logic.
  • OData V4: Widely adopted for RESTful APIs, offering improved support for complex data structures and enhanced security.
  • Interoperability: Easily consumed by various clients and platforms, promoting integration of different systems.
  • Scalability and Performance: Designed for large-scale applications with efficient data handling and clustering support.
  • Developer Productivity: Provides UI controls, model-driven development, and flexible data retrieval, enhancing productivity.

Key Features

With this 2408 release for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, we deliver the basic scope for the following interface:

Sales Contracts OData V4 API

  • Provide the initial version of the sales contract OData V4 API to create, update, read, and delete the sales contract and its entities, including:
  • Sales contract header, Sales contract item
  • Price elements (sales contract header and item)
  • Text (sales contract header and item)
  • Use the sales contract OData V4 API together with existing sales contract APIs to fulfill different integration use cases to operate sales contracts in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

For more information, see Sales Contract (A2X, OData V4).

Preliminary Billing Document OData v4 API

This OData V4 API, which offers both synchronous and asynchronous processing, makes the data of preliminary billing documents available through its header, item, partner, and text entities. You can extend it with custom fields via key user extensibility.

With this new 2408 release, we deliver the basic scope of the preliminary billing document OData v4 API for managing preliminary billing documents:

  • Operations: read and update
  • Actions: CreateFromSDDocument, reject, SetStatusToFinalized, SetStatusToInProgress, and WithdrawFromApproval
  • Entities: header, item, partner, and text
  • Custom field extensibility 

For more information, see APIs for Preliminary Billing Document.

Billing Document Request OData v4 API

This SOAP-based web service enables external consumers to create billing document requests by sending billing data into your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. Once the data is persisted in the form of billing document requests, it can be converged with billing data from other internal and external sources to create combined customer invoices. These can be used to invoice customers for a variety of products and services within a single document.

Whit this new 2408 release, we deliver the basic scope of the billing document request OData v4 API for managing billing document requests:

  • Operations: read and delete
  • Actions: CreateFromExternalData and reject
  • Entities: header, item, partner, and text 

For more information, see APIs for Billing Document Request.

Billing document OData v4 API

Billing documents collectively refer to customer invoices, credit memos, debit memos, pro forma invoices, and related cancellation documents. This inbound OData v4 service makes billing document data in your system available through its header, item, and partner entities.

With this new 2408 release, we deliver the basic scope of the billing document OData v4 API to operate billing documents, including basic billing document entities:

  • Operations: read and update
  • Actions: CreateFromSDDocument, CreateCancellationDocument, and PostToAccounting
  • Entities: header, item, partner, and text
  • Custom field extensibility 

For more information, see APIs for Billing Document.

Returns Order OData v4 API

You can use the OData V4 API Customer Return (A2X) to manage standard customer returns by creating, reading, and updating returns orders for order type CBAR from external systems. In addition, you can delete single entities (such as items) in return orders.

You can choose whether you want to process your requests in synchronous or asynchronous mode.

With this new 2408 release, we deliver the basic scope of the Returns Order OData v4 API to operate returns orders, including basic returns order entities:

  • Returns order header,
  • Returns order items,
  • Pricing elements (header and item),
  • Partner entities (header and item),
  • Text (header and item).

For more information, see Customer Return (A2X, OData V4).

 Picture 5: Leverage the new features offered in the ABAP RESTful application programming model framework.

Remark

Stay tuned on the progress of this function as in future releases, SAP will continuously add more features and enhancements here.

 

Early Adopter Care Service

SAP offers a range of programs that allow customers to influence SAP software development decisions and adopt innovations early on.

And here we are searching for customers who want to evaluate the Assisted Object Creation of Customer Return.

Here you can test the following new features and give your feedback to SAP, which allows you to influence the features for customer returns.

The new features for evaluating the Assisted Object Creation of Customer Returns are:

You can create return orders with the aid of input recommendations.

  • Based on the data you’ve entered in the returns order; the system provides input recommendations for related fields.
  • For example, when you enter a sold-to party, the system automatically recommends the best-matched returns order reason.
  • When you enter the product, the system recommends the return reason, supplier, refund data, and other related information.

 Picture 6: Register and experience the latest AI features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and influence upcoming AI developments.

For more information, see Get Involved Early and Customer Returns Influence Program.

Remark

Beta testing provides you with early access to SAP software to test and see how it fits your business requirements. Beta shipments are provided under a Test and Evaluation Agreement (TEA) and can be used for testing purposes only. Productive usage is not allowed and is not supported under the maintenance and support agreement.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

For more information about Sales, please see the Link Collection for Sales here

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

 

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series!

You will find a 50-minute-long recording and a presentation for Sales as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: GRC, Trade, and Tax with SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408
entry.content:
Hello and welcome to the release highlights for GRC, Trade and Tax with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408. This blog contains selected highlights from International Trade, Core Tax, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, as well as SAP Risk and Assurance Management and SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance along with their business benefits and deep-dive system demos.

To get a quick overview of our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 highlights for GRC, Trade and Tax, watch this 3-minute video:

 

Topics of Covered in this Blog

International Trade

  • Integration of SAP Watch List Screening with Business Partner Master Data

Tax

  • AI-Assisted Configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions

  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Mass Processing of Reporting Tasks
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Electronic Invoices in Malaysia

GRC

  • SAP Risk and Assurance Management: Automatic Issue owner Determination, Notification and Assignment

  • SAP Risk and Assurance Management: Ad-Hoc Tasks in Issue and Remediation Management

  • SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Enhanced Access Request Workflow

  • SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Fine-Grained Authorization for Privileged Access Management
  • SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Extension of Rule Content

 

International Trade

Integration of SAP Watch List Screening with Business Partner Master Data

SAP Watch List Screening is part of international trade compliance and checks business transactions such as sales orders and deliveries against sanctioned party lists to prevent high-risk business with sanctioned persons and organizations. As of this release, you can also screen business partners in the role of customers and suppliers. SAP Watch List Screening runs on SAP BTP and is integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. ​

Value Proposition​

  • Prevent high-risk business with sanctioned persons and organizations​
  • Assure legal compliance regarding sanctioned-party-list screening for customer and supplier master data​

Capabilities​

  • Screen names and addresses of business partners in the role of customer or supplier​
  • Call the SAP Watch List Screening application on the SAP Business Technology Platform from within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition​
  • ‘Maintain Business Partner’ app:​
  • Add status fields representing the screening status of the addresses of business partners​
  • Manage a cumulative status field on the level of general data of the business partner​
  • Consider business partner screening results during business document creation and update

 

​Video 2: As of 2408, it is now possible to screen business partners in the role of customer or supplier with SAP Watch List Screening in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

​More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Integration with SAP Watch List Screening

Back to top

 

Tax

AI-Assisted Configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions

This feature is currently in beta testing.

Feature Overview​

If you’re using the internal UJS tax jurisdiction solution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can now maintain your jurisdiction codes and rates with just three simple steps.​

  • From a dozen steps down to three: Use a natural-language prompt to retrieve detailed information from the AI, then verify, simulate, and save your configured tax jurisdiction codes and rates. ​
  • Once you’ve configured the connection between your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system and the LLM, you can chat directly with the AI to retrieve detailed address-based tax jurisdiction information. ​
  • The LLM also returns a link to the respective state tax or revenue authority, giving you an easy way to verify your tax rate information before simulating and saving your changes. 

 

​Video 3: Currently in beta testing: The AI-Assisted Configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions

Register and experience the latest AI features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and influence upcoming AI developments.

More Information

  • ​Get Involved Early

Back to top

 

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Mass Processing of Reporting Tasks

Businesses need to create reports to be submitted to the tax authorities across countries resulting in hundreds of reports per month. Instead of creating them one by one, business users can accelerate statutory reporting using the mass processing of reporting tasks in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

Value Proposition

  • Reduce manual effort for creation and submission of statutory reports
  • Lower the risk of noncompliance due to missed deadlines
  • Enhance the overall business user experience by reducing user interventions required during individual report processing

Capabilities

  • Mass processing of reports by allowing reporting actions such as report run creation
  • Centralized progress log indicating the overall status of the mass action as well as details of processing and results of the action for each report
  • Ability to perform report-level checks such as authorization for each report during the mass action processing
  • Parallel processing of mass actions across reports

Fig. 1: As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402, business users can accelerate statutory reporting using the mass processing of reporting tasks in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Mass Action

Back to top

 

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Electronic Invoices in Malaysia

As of August 2024, Malaysia authorities are introducing mandatory electronic invoices. The country wants to promote electronic invoicing in business transactions between businesses. Electronic invoicing is a measure for digitizing business relationships, reducing transaction costs, and increasing transparency in commercial traffic.  With SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, as of 2408, you can seamlessly generate and electronically exchange invoices through authorities’ portal.

Value Proposition

  • Enable comprehensive compliance with new regulations for electronic invoices in Malaysia
  • Deliver a consistent user experience across countries and business processes
  • Simplify implementation of new local requirements
  • Automate and simplify invoicing processes

Capabilities

  • Support validation through authorities’ portal of
  • Customer invoices
  • Buyer-issued invoices
  • Refund notes
  • Buyer rejections

Fig. 2: As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402, you can seamlessly generate and electronically exchange invoices through authorities’ portal in Malaysia in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Document and Reporting Compliance

Back to top

 

GRC

SAP Risk and Assurance Management

SAP Risk and Assurance Management is integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition and provides you with the tools to become compliant with internal controls, laws, and regulations that apply to your organization. In Issue Management and Remediation, you can process and monitor issues resulting from manual or automated procedures in Issue Management and Remediation.

 

Automatic Issue Owner Determination, Notification and Assignment

Now, you benefit from automatic dispatching of new issues to issue owners, email notifications for issue nominees, and automatic issue owner assignments.

Value Proposition

  • Enable automatic dispatching of new issues to issue owners
  • Enable email notifications to make nominees aware of new issues
  • Avoid orphaned issues by automatic issue owner assignment after a grace period
  • Help issues find their best processor under consideration of absences, workload, and so on
  • Provide clear notification status for each issue available

Capabilities

  • Automatic determination of issue owner nominees based on business data
  • Email notifications with direct link to the issue for all nominees
  • Category-specific reminder email notification after a configurable period of time
  • Fallback mechanism to assign a default user after a configurable grace period
  • Dashboard provides easy access for issue nominees

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Notification of Nominees
  • SAP Help Portal: and How to Set Up Automatic Issue Owner Assignment (Admin Guide)

 

Video 3: Now, compliance specialists benefit from automatic dispatching of new issues to issue owners, email notifications for issue nominees, and automatic issue owner assignments in SAP Risk and Assurance Management

Back to top

 

Ad-Hoc Tasks in Issue Management and Remediation

As of this release, you can create user-defined tasks on ad-hoc basis and complement existing task lists.

Value Proposition

  • Get a flexible extension of task lists by issue owner​
  • Address topics using tasks that have not been foreseen at design time​
  • Avoid communication outside the Process Issues app in order to address issue-specific topics

Capabilities

Ad-hoc tasks allowing users to create tasks at runtime that are not provided through predefined task list templates:

  • Create user-defined tasks on an ad-hoc basis
  • Complement predefined task lists with tailored tasks

Video 4: Now, you can create user-defined tasks on ad-hoc basis and complement existing task lists in SAP Risk and Assurance Management

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Creating Tasks Manually

Back to top

 

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Enhanced Access Request Workflow

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, access request service is a cloud service for creating self-service requests to applications for on-premise and cloud source applications and systems. Thanks to the new approach of a dynamic risk owner determinator, you can dynamically determine the current risk owner stage approver. This determinator assigns the risk owner stage approver based on business rules that evaluate both risk and user attributes when an access request is created.

Value Proposition

  • Gain additional flexibility to define a more streamlined access approval process based on various business needs

Capabilities

  • Additional optional configurations in the access request workflow:
  • Dynamically identify the risk approver in the approval process
  • Based on configuration setting allow access-risk mitigation during the approval process

Fig. 3: Thanks to the enhanced access request workflow, you can dynamically identify the risk approver in the access request approval process

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Dynamical Risk Owner Determinator

Back to top

 

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Fine-Grained Authorization for Privileged Access Management

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance, privileged access management service is a cloud solution that lets you create self-service requests for emergency access to systems and applications. Thanks to this innovation, you can facilitate granular authorization roles by application.

Value Proposition

  • Enhance security and control by helping ensure that users have access only to the applications necessary for their roles
  • Reduce the risk of unauthorized access and potential security breaches
  • Align with the principle of least privilege, and improve overall compliance with security policies and regulations

Capabilities

  • Granting access to each application within the privileged access management (PAM) service, through distinct roles in SAP Business Technology Platform, facilitating granular authorization roles

Fig. 4: Thanks to the fine-grained authorizations for Privileged Access Management, you can facilitate granular authorization roles by application

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: Pre-delivered role collections on BTP

Back to top

 

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance: Extension of Rule Content

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance is integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition and supports comprehensive identity management and access governance. It allows you to minimize access risk and mitigate potential risks for financial loss.

Value Proposition

  • Support continuous compliance to access policies through provision of out of the box rule set to assess access risks
  • Upgrading applications may introduce new roles, permissions, and potential conflicts. Ensures access risk rule sets are updated to maintain alignment between access risk analysis assessments and the evolving landscape.

Capabilities

  • Updated pre-delivered rule set content for additional scenarios, including:
  • Segregation of risks
  • Critical-access risks

Fig. 5: The extension of rule content ensures that access risk rule sets are updated to maintain alignment between access risk analysis assessments and the evolving landscape

More Information

  • SAP Help Portal: What’s New

Back to top 

 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

entry.title: Media Product Hierarchy with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Co-authored by Mandheer Walia and Christiane Erzgraeber.

Media companies want to report financial figures by title. Title in this case means any type of content (physical or digital) represented by a media product or a group of products that are structured in a product hierarchy. Examples can be

  • Series --> Season --> Episodes and Merchandise
  • Digital or Physical publications: Publication --> Edition --> Issue  
  • Any type of digital content e.g., Games, Journal, Assessment, ….

What’s unique about the industry is that, in the product hierarchy, a product can be assigned to any level or node of the hierarchy. In addition, several products can be assigned to a sub node in the hierarchy, but each media product has only one superordinate product. Lastly, there is support for multiple hierarchies e.g., content hierarchy, merchandize, ….

The generic capabilities of product hierarchies are documented in SAP Help Portal

To support the special requirements of media and entertainment companies for product hierarchy, a new hierarchy type - Product Hierarchy – Type 2 is available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

How to create a Media Product Hierarchy

To create and edit hierarchies use app “Manage Product Hierarchies” (App ID: F4359) and make sure that role Master Data Specialist – Product (SAP_BR_PRODMASTER_SPECIALIST) is assigned to your user.

In the entry screen you can see a list of all existing hierarchies in your system. Choose “create”.

 

In window “New Hierarchy” choose “Product Hierarchy - Type 2” and fill in values for all other required fields.

The hierarchy ID represents the level 1 entry or root note for your hierarchy. All further nodes are created as child of this ID. You can restrict the validity period of your product hierarchy.

Press “create” to continue.

After initial creation you are back in the overview screen. Next you need to maintain nodes and assign products. To do so, make sure that you are in edit mode of your hierarchy.

You can maintain your hierarchy either manually or import / export from spreadsheet. For manual maintenance, mark your product hierarchy and click on either “Add” or “Fast Entry” on the right side of the screen.

The user can decide whether to create a subnode or a product node. A product can be assigned at either, subnode level or product node level, but the difference is that a subnode can always have further nodes whereas a product node is always the lowest node in that hierarchy.

In our example, we first want to create a subnode that represents series “Underworld”. The required product master records are already created in the system.  Enter the respective product ID.

By expanding value help for field “Node” you can search for products. Mark the right one and click “OK”.

Series “Underworld” is now assigned to product hierarchy “CONTENT_HIERARCHY”. Don’t forget to save your changes.

Next, we want to assign the first season to our series. Open the hierarchy in edit mode and choose “Add”. Create a subnode to the series and search for the product that represents the first season.

Finally, we need to assign the episodes to our season. For the episodes we are pretty sure that they will be the lowest level in our hierarchy, so that we can create them as product. We have four episodes and want to create them using the fast entry function.

Mark “Product” and make use of the search help for the products to be assigned.

When finished press “OK”.

Back in the hierarchy overview press “Save”.

You now have created a media product hierarchy that is the basis in use cases where you want to report  Title means not only a single product but also parts or the complete hierarchy you have set up. The product hierarchy can be consumed by apps like “Trial Balance”, and it is possible to drill down to all levels in the hierarchy. We will cover this in a separate blog.

entry.title: We might not know how to solve your problems, but we recommend using our FMEA app to prevent them
entry.content:
🤷 FMEA? At SAP? Never heard of it…

If you’re asking yourself what an FMEA is, please check out this earlier blog post: Quality Management in S/4HANA Cloud – Failure Mode and Effect Analysis (FMEA).   

If you're already familiar with FMEAs but you'd like to take a glimpse at the new SAP Fiori app for FMEAs in ‘SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition’ check out this blog post about new features in the Manage FMEAs app with 2302. If videos are more your thing, check out this video introducing the graphical user interface of the Manage FMEAs app.

Manage FMEAs app in Quality Management

🤩 Manage FMEAs – the SAP Fiori app in Quality Management now S/4HANA home page

Since 2308, the app is available as a tile on the SAP S/4HANA home page (aka SAP Fiori launchpad). We take this exciting fact as a discussion starter for this blog post. The Manage FMEAs app is oriented towards the discrete industry and its standards. For certified companies in the discrete industry, FMEAs are a must.

🙄 I don’t do FMEAs at all

You’re still waiting for defects and complaints to happen and keep on adding characteristics to your inspection plan as you go? You must be a rich company!

FMEAs are done to prevent failures from happening. It’s way cheaper to think through all possible failures in an FMEA before you start with production. For each function, your team thinks deeply about possible problems and comes up with the right characteristics to include in quality inspection and the right measures for prevention and early detection.

🤓 I do FMEAs in spreadsheets

You’re trying to squeeze a net structure into a tree table? You’re working with text only? An FMEA session is actually hard work for all participants, so why not add at least a bit of fun: You’ll just love the graphical UI and real net visualization of the FMEA app in ‘SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition’. Forget confusing text trees: With the graphical net, you define and visualize elements, functions, effects, failures, and causes with their connections.

Top: Manage FMEAs app – graphical net view. Bottom: example of an FMEA in a table

In addition, a spreadsheet is practically the opposite of integration. To optimize your FMEA, you want to define and monitor detection and preventive actions within the context of your causes and failures – a spreadsheet is too disconnected and static to support here. Using the Manage FMEAs app and the FMEA cockpit:

  • Your team defines and documents the characteristics to be included in the inspection plan or routing.
  • The detection and preventive actions are ready to be executed in a worklist with status handling. This way you keep track of your progress. 
  • Your team can easily write off all detection and preventive actions and instantly evaluate how likely causes are to occur and to be detected in the context of actions.
  • Based on this data, the system calculates the action priority for each FMEA cause and element. This shows you which causes need your attention and action definition most. You can also sort FMEA elements by action priority to obtain a prioritized worklist.

Although our workplaces are mostly digitized, we all know that some paperwork still has to happen at certain interfaces – for FMEAs, that’s for example the interface between customer and supplier. Here, PDF forms are still used to exchange selected information. Our app offers you to download an FMEA form that is oriented towards VDA standard.

🤯 I do FMEAs in a disconnected third-party tool 

I feel with you. I know, some see it as an advantage to work in “their own tool” in the quality department – not having to deal or even collaborate with the production.  

But let's face it: Collaboration is key, the integration of tools makes things transparent, and overall, working with our integrated tools makes processes reliable and safe. You enjoy the security of one single source of truth: your SAP system.

If quality management and production both happen in the same tool, you practically force your staff to collaborate and enjoy the transparency. Let’s put it that way: We, at SAP, bring your departments closer together. Quality, Research & Development, and Production will join forces and collaborate closer.  

Creating FMEAs with the Manage FMEAs app has the advantage of integration. You can, for example, add your material or operation at the FMEA element in our cockpit. There you can also create characteristics and reuse them for other FMEAs. Information should not lie around in disconnected third-party tools because that’s often not only time-consuming but also makes processes instable and error-prone.

📬 You like FMEAs as much as we do? And use ‘SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition’ productively? Contact us, give feedback and chat!

entry.title: Where can I deepen my expertise on sales solution processes to get ready for the certification?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sales Automation, Analytics and Configuration learning journey.

Overview
Within this Learning Journey, SAP application consultants can deepen their expertise of sales solution processes, specifically focusing on Automation, Analytics and Configuration.

This course is part of the series “Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sales”, which includes courses on the following subjects:
  1. Sales Fundamental Business Processes
  2. Sales Complaint Processing
  3. Sales Advanced Business Processes
  4. Sales Automation, Analytics and Configuration

Learning objectives
Consultants will achieve the following key learning objectives:

  • Proficiency in Sales Processes: Master the execution and management of additional solution processes, including convergent billing, sales orders monitoring, AI-based sales order entry, sales planning, predictive analytics.
  • Practical Application: Develop hands-on experience through exercises in a real instance of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Prerequisites

  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sales Fundamental Business Processes
Book your certification examValidate your skills and learning by completing the certification exam and gain a highly recognised accreditation.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Test Automation Tool in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
A major theme for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Test Automation Tool Public Edition, over the past year, was to enable customers to do automation testing for all the extensions they build as part of the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Implementation. With the 2408 release we complete this arc of development. 


Support for Custom API

Starting with the 2408 release, custom API’s created as part of Key User Extensibility and Developer Extensibility(Embedded Steampunk) facets of SAP S/4HANA Cloud can also be tested in the Test Automation Tool. 

Custom API Support in API Automation Framework

This feature enables our end users to cover even custom API's created via extensions. The process is similar to the way we create SOAP and OData API's we create from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. Communication arrangement and user created for the API can be re-used in the tool as well.

ABAP Units Dashboard Tile in AATR

ABAP Units Dashboard for PUT Executions

2402 release enabled customers to add ABAP Units to Post Upgrade Tests(PUT) as well. While in the previous release the only way to access the results were through the "Track PUT Results" pop-up immediately after the PUT run, we now persist the data in the ABAP Units dashboard created specifically for PUT executions in 3SL systems. 

The results now can be accessed in the Analyse Automated Test Results(AATR) applications, details about the error reason, and coverage statistics from ABAP Development Tool(ADT) is provided. 

ABAP Units Detail View

Additional Innovations

  • Enhanced Auto-binding for Test Data Container(TDC)
    The auto-binding feature is enhanced, to utilize additional intelligence available from the Object Repository(OR), to match fields even when they do not have an exact text match. Maintenance and setting up of TDC is further simplified with this approach
  • Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) Advanced Filtering
    i-TS application filters can now be used to further refine optimized scope and create Execution Variants in the Manage Upgrade Tests(MUT) application. Optimized scope can be defined based on Application Area (Finance, Sales etc.) or even Scope Item (BDG, BD9, J11 etc.) and selected for Post Upgrade Test(PUT) execution.
    Intelligent Test Scoper(i-TS) Application

     

  • Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) Scope Efficiency Indicator
    The scope efficiency gained with each update or upgrade can be seen using the "View Scope Efficiency" button in the application. 

SAP Delivered Standard Automates

The total list of SAP delivered automated test scripts are 443 for 2408 and can be accessed via link – Automated Test Scripts Index”. This includes 27 new scope items which were added this release, detail below:

R&D/Engineering :  
Authoring of Product Compliance Documents (7DO)
Retail :
Product Taxonomy Management (5FJ), Inbound Processing Distribution Center (5FU), 
Outbound Processing Distribution Center (5FV), Replenishment Planning - Store (3I7), 
Replenishment Planning - Distribution Center (3I8), Location Management (3I3), 
Empties Management / Processing (5FK), Retail Logistics with Embedded Warehouse Management (69L), 
Seasons for Retail (6DI), Merchandise Distribution and Collective Purchase order (71O), 
Assortments (3I5)

Further detailed change information about the standard automate is available in the “Automated Test Script Change History” and “Test Script Release Change Information”.

Important Links and Updates

We have created a new Learning Hub course for an overview of the tool. This is a good starting point for anyone who is new to the tool, or want to get an overview of the new features we have added over the past couple of years. [Before you can access the link, a one-time registration in the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by-step guidance can be found here]

As part of our PUT 2.0 Enablement also we have created a set of short videos which cover the most important aspects of the process. The video playlist can be accessed here.

Links to other important blogs:

  • How to get started with the Test Automation Tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Release by Bert Schulze

Please bookmark the test automation community page to keep up with the latest updates happening in the test automation space.

For more details on the functionality please refer the links below:

Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) 
Manage Upgrade Tests 
Manage Your Test Processes
Test Your Processes
Test Data Container
Analyze Automated Test Results
Configure Conditional Authentication
Also refer FAQs

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
