entry.title: Product Compliance in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
Safety Data Sheets (SDS) serve as a communication instrument throughout the supply chain, ensuring that relevant stakeholders have access to accurate and consistent information about chemical hazards. Suppliers, distributors, and end-users rely on SDS to make informed decisions about safely handling, storing, and using chemicals. Learn more about how the innovative Generation of Product Compliance Documents allows the creation of SDS using standard document tools from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Additionally, I will walk you through my selected key highlights for Product Compliance:

To name just a few highlights:

  • Regulatory Content Updates for Product Marketability, Dangerous Goods Management, and Safety Data Sheets,
  • Generation of Product Compliance Documents with Integration into the Safety Data Sheet Process,
  • New SAP Fiori apps for Safety Data Sheet,
  • Mass Classification of Packaged Dangerous Goods,
  • Mixed Loading Check Rules for IMDG 2022,
  • … and more.

In case you do not need all the information below and briefly want to get an overview of the release highlights, please watch this video with the top three key innovations:

Release video: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 for Product Compliance: Top 3 key innovations.

 

Regulatory Content Updates

Business Background

In the area of product compliance, the importance of content is crucial. Ensuring that products meet their target markets' strict legal and regulatory standards is essential, covering areas such as safety, environmental impact, and labeling guidelines. As businesses navigate the complex world of compliance, having access to comprehensive content becomes vital.

In this blog post, we will explore the significant role of content in product compliance processes, highlighting how it supports compliance with regulations and facilitates smooth operations. We will specifically focus on SAP's provision of essential content for various compliance elements, including compliance requirements, graphics, regulatory phrases with translations, listed substances, substance groups, and substance conditions. Join us as we uncover the significance of content in driving excellence in product compliance and aligning with regulations.

Key Feature

With this new 2408 release, SAP delivers new and updated compliance requirements for Product Marketability, Safety Data Sheet Management, and Dangerous Goods Management, which you can use in your product compliance processes.

 Picture 1: Stay compliant with your product portfolio using the most recent regulatory updates.

Regulatory Content for Product Marketability

W Content update that includes 25 new compliance requirement versions:

  • AU AIIC status (April 2024)
  • CA DSL+NDSL status (March 2024)
  • EU ECHA CoRAP status (March 2024)
  • JP CSCL class 1 and 2 status (March 2024)
  • JP CSCL general chemicals (April 2024)
  • JP CSCL PACS status (April 2024)
  • JP ENCS status (March 2024)
  • JP ISHA status (March 2024)
  • KR CCA toxic list status (February 2024)
  • NL ZZS list status (March 2024)
  • NO priority substances (April 2024)
  • NZ NZIOC status (March 2024)
  • SV drug precursors (February 2024)
  • TH narcotic drugs (January 2024)
  • US California SCPR (March 2024)
  • US TSCA export notification (April 2024)
  • US TSCA inventory status (February 2024)
  • US TSCA section 6 status (March 2024)
  • US TSCA SNUR status (April 2024)
  • VN conditional chemicals (March 2024)
  • VN chemical weapons convention (March 2024)
  • VN declarable chemicals (March 2024)
  • VN restricted chemicals (March 2024)
  • World GADSL status (February 2024)
  • JP CSCL exempt status (March 2024)

Note: In total, 150 compliance requirements for product marketability 

Regulatory Content Updates for Safety Data Sheets, including Labeling and Occupational Exposure Limits

New compliance requirements for the following:

  • DE water hazard class (April 2017)
  • NL water hazard class (March 2016)
  • Ch water hazard class (January 2019)
  • World handling and storage (July 2023)

New compliance requirement versions for the following:

  • EU GHS classification (January 2024)
  • EU GHS labeling (January 2024)
  • EU GHS consumer labeling (January 2024)
  • World GHS classification (July 2023)
  • World GHS consumer labeling (July 2023)
  • World GHS labeling (July 2023)
  • BR components with OELs (July 2023)
  • BR hazardous ingredients (July 2023)
  • World UN hazardous ingredients (July 2023)
  • Germany: TRGS occupational exposure limits (January 2024)
  • Spain: occupational exposure limits (January 2024)
  • EU: exposure to carcinogens or mutagens
    at work – 2014/27/EU (March 2024)
  • EU: indicative occupational exposure limits (March 2024)
  • Finland: OELs for carcinogenic substances (March 2024)
  • France: occupational exposure limits (April 2024)
  • Ireland: occupational exposure limits (April 2024)
  • Lithuania: occupational exposure limits (January 2024)
  • Norway; occupational exposure limits (April 2024)
  • Denmark: occupational exposure limits (March 2024) 

In total, we have

  • 102 compliance requirements for safety data sheet management,
  • 113 compliance requirements for safety data sheet authoring,
  • and 92 compliance requirements for occupational exposure limits.

Regulatory Content Update for Dangerous Goods Management: United Nations Dangerous Goods Transport Recommendations

New compliance requirement version for:

  • UNRTDG 2024

In total, embedding of 15 compliance requirements for dangerous goods management:

  • UNRTDG
  • IMDG Code
  • IATA DGR
  • ADR
  • RID
  • ADN
  • 49 CFRx
  • TDG
  • NOM
  • ANTT
  • NCh 382
  • NZS 5433
  • SANS 10228
  • ADG
  • JT/T617.3 

Enhanced Dangerous Goods Lists – Overview

ADN :

  • Equipment Required (9)
  • Ventilation (10)
  • Mixed Packing Provision (9b)
  • Special Provision for Carriage, Packages (16)

ADR:

  • Mixed Packing Provision (9b)
  • Vehicle for Tank Carriage (14)
  • Special Provision for Carriage, Packages (16)
  • Special Provision for Carriage, Bulk (17)

RID:

  • Mixed Packing Provision (9b)
  • Special Provision for Carriage, Packages (16)
  • Special Provision for Carriage, Bulk (17)

CFR 49:

  • Vessel Stowage Location (10A)
  • Vessel Stowage Other (10B)

TDG:

  • ERAP Index (7)
  • Passenger Carrying Index (8/9)

 Demo 1: Ensure your product portfolio remains compliant by staying updated with the latest regulatory changes.

For more information, see:

  • SAP Content for Product Marketability and Chemical Compliance.
  • SAP Content for Dangerous Goods Management.
  • SAP Content for Safety Data Sheet Management.

 

Generation of Product Compliance Documents with Integration into the Safety Data Sheet Process

Business Background

A safety data sheet (SDS) must be sent to industry customers at the time of delivery when hazardous substances or mixtures are sold. Safety data sheets are globally the central document for communication on hazardous substances or mixtures that are toxic, corrosive, ignitable, explosive, oxidizing, chemically reactive, or hazardous to health or the environment. Example products: cleaner, paint, pyrotechnic articles for vehicles, batteries (US), biocides, pesticides, oil, gas, metal powder, plastic granulate, and candles.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SDS Management is integrated into checks documents in the value chain, such as sales orders, to avoid the sale of products in markets where the necessary safety data sheets are unavailable.

Until now, if a safety data sheet was missing, the only option was to upload an existing safety data sheet that had been generated by an external system.

Key Feature

Now with this new 2408 release, we made the first step concerning the SDS generation you can generate a first selection of sections of a European safety data sheet in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for product compliance solution using Adobe Document Services.

In SAP S/4HANA, Adobe LiveCycle Designer (LD) and Adobe Document Services (ADS) are the only supported services for layouts and generating safety data sheet documents.

In the “Manage Safety Data Sheets” app, you can choose Generate to generate a base safety data sheet for all languages of a safety data sheet assessment. This generation process is run by ADS. A final safety data sheet is automatically generated during the safety data sheet shipment when the safety data sheet assessment containing a base safety data sheet is released.

If you want to adopt this final safety data sheet, SAP delivers two Business Add-Ins (BAdIs) that enable you to gather data and map it to form templates:

  • Adaption of Content for Product Compliance Documents
    (BADI_EHFND_DATA_PRVDR_ADAPTION) enables you to determine data that is used to generate product-compliance-related documents.
  • Adaption of Data Mapping for Safety Data Sheet Generation
    (BADI_EHSDS_GEN_DATA_STRUCTURE) enables you to map safety-data-sheet-related data into a format that is used in the form data provider to generate a safety data sheet document.

 Demo 2: Create product compliance documents using standard document tools from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public edition.

Restriction

It is currently only possible to generate specific sections of a safety data sheet for EU countries. SAP only delivers the required labels and heading phrases for Germany (DE). If you want to generate further sections, use the two BAdIs to gather and map the required data.

If you want to generate a safety data sheet for other EU countries/regions, you have to link the corresponding template to the compliance requirement version and manually create the phrases.

If you want to generate a safety data sheet for countries/regions outside the EU, you have to create a new template, collect and map the data accordingly, and link the template to the compliance requirement version.

 For more information, see:

  • Sections of the Safety Data Sheet
  • Authoring of Safety Data Sheets and Labels.
  • Create product compliance documents using standard document tools.
  • BAdI: Adaption of Data Mapping for Safety Data Sheet Generation.

 

New SAP Fiori apps for Safety Data Sheet

Business Background

Related to the previous chapter concerning the Generation of Product Compliance Documents with Integration into the Safety Data Sheet Process, we have also introduced new SAP Fiori apps.

New SAP Fiori app for Management of Identifiers

With this new 2408 release, you can use the “Manage Identifiers” app to display, change, add, and delete names and numbers that are used to identify unpackaged products and raw materials. You can use these identifiers, such as the IUPAC name or CAS registry number, to connect products and raw materials with regulatory substance databases.

These identifiers also serve as tags to help you search for unpackaged products in the “Manage Basic Compliance Data - Unpackaged Products” (F2641) app so that you can analyze and work on them. You can also print these identifiers so that they can be used in safety data sheets and labels.

 Picture 2: Manage identifiers such as CAS number or IUPAC name for chemical products.

For more information, see Manage Identifiers.

New SAP Fiori app for Stability and Reactivity

With this new 2408 release, you can enter and edit information about the stability and reactivity of an unpackaged product or raw material in the new “Manage Stability and Reactivity” (F7930) app.

This allows you to enter the information separately from the safety-related properties at a later point in time. The information about stability and reactivity is required in section 10 of a safety data sheet and can be used for the classification of products according to different hazardous substances or dangerous goods regulations.

 Picture 3: New SAP Fiori app to maintain data for SDS section 10 - stability and reactivity.

For more information, refer to the following app descriptions:

  • For unpackaged products: Stability and Reactivity
  • For raw materials: Stability and Reactivity

New SAP Fiori app for Handling and Storage

With this new 2408 release, you can enter handling and storage data for a raw material or an unpackaged product in the “Manage Handling and Storage” (F7929) app. You can update the data to ensure that it stays compliant over time. You can also indicate where the data you’ve entered can be used, for example, for public use on safety data sheets.

When you have provided the handling and storage information, you can release it for further use. Information about handling and storage is required for section 7 of a safety data sheet.

 Picture 4: New SAP Fiori app to maintain data for SDS section 7 – handling and storage.

For more information, see Manage Handling and Storage.

New SAP Fiori app for Water Hazard Class

Legal Requirements: In Germany, the Netherlands, and Switzerland, substances and mixtures are classified according to their hazard potential to the environment and human health into water hazard classes. Information about water hazard classes is often included in section 15 of a German, Dutch, or Swiss safety data sheet.

With this new 2408 release, you can assess products, raw materials, and substances according to the applicable water hazard class regulations and manage the compliance assessments.

With this new SAP Fiori “Manage Water Hazard Class” (F5756) app, the following compliance requirement versions for the water hazard class assessments are provided with the SAP regulatory content:

  • CH Water Hazard Class (Jan 2019)
  • DE Water Hazard Class (WGK) (Apr 2017)
  • NL Water Hazard Class (GAM (Mar 2016)

The delivered compliance requirement versions are based on the Regulatory Data Assessment compliance pattern.

The assessments that you can carry out according to water hazard class regulations are based on these delivered compliance requirement versions.

Picture 5: New SAP Fiori app to maintain data for SDS section 15 – water hazard class.

For more information, see Manage Water Hazard Class.

 

Mass Classification of Packaged Dangerous Goods

Business Background

As a hazardous materials expert, your responsibility is to assess products that are intended for transportation. If the product is considered to be dangerous goods, you must classify it according to the applicable dangerous goods regulations. Additionally, you must determine whether the product can be transported in a particular country or region using a specific mode of transportation.

With the Dangerous Goods Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can:

  • Evaluate both unpackaged and packaged items,
  • Categorize them based on relevant dangerous goods regulations,
  • Determine if their transportation is permissible, subject to restrictions, or prohibited.

Key Feature

With this new 2408 release, we introduce the Dangerous Goods Classification of Selected Products.

With this new feature, you can select multiple products and classify all the selected products together instead of having to classify them one by one.

Here we have 2 use cases:

  • If you have selected products that are classified as no dangerous goods, the system releases the classification of the selected products and sets the transport permission to Allowed for all modes of transport.
  • In the second use case, if you have selected products that are classified as dangerous goods, a new classification dialog opens in which you can enter the required information for the dangerous goods package. You then select the enclosure variant, and, if applicable, assign an exemption category for the selected products. The system then determines the applicable dangerous goods classification. You can verify the classification data and save or release the classification assessments for all selected products in one step.

 Demo 3: Use the mass functionality “Classify Selected Products” in the “Classify Unpackaged Dangerous Goods” app by selecting multiple packaged dangerous goods in the section “Related Packaged Products”.

For more information, see:

  • How to classify multiple selected products in one go, see Classifying Packaged Products Using the My Packaged Dangerous Goods app.
  • How to classify packaged products according to the applicable dangerous goods regulations, see Classifying Packaged Dangerous Goods.

 

Mixed Loading Check Rules for IMDG 2022

Business Background

In the world of transporting dangerous goods, there are strict rules that state certain hazardous items cannot be loaded together in the same transport vehicle. To maintain safety regulations and ensure adherence, an important procedure called a mixed loading check is utilized. This involves confirming that different hazardous materials are compatible, allowing only those that can be transported together to be loaded. Carrying out a comprehensive mixed loading check assists experts in reducing risks, avoiding accidents, and upholding regulatory compliance.

Key Feature

With this new 2408 release, a mixed loading check is provided that determines whether compliance-relevant products can be transported together in the same transport unit or must be segregated.

 Picture 6: Additional messages generated for mixed loading.

The mixed loading check can be enabled using the Enable Mixed Loading Check configuration activity. If enabled, the check determines the dangerous goods classification data for the products that are relevant for the check and compares the data with segregation rules that are specified in the Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations (BADI_EHDGM_MIXED_LOAD) Business Add-In (BAdI). This BAdI is released for developer extensibility. You can use it to influence the behavior of the mixed loading check by specifying specific segregation rules based on segregation codes or classes.

You can view the results of the mixed loading check using the “Dangerous Goods Information - For the Value Chain” (F7369) app.

 Demo 4: Check if your business documents have incompatibilities according to IMDG 2022.

Capabilities

Mixed loading check rules for IMDG 2022 supporting:

  • Segregation groups of chapter 7.2.5
  • Segregation table of chapter 7.2.4
  • Segregation table of chapter 7.2.7
  • Segregation codes of chapter 7.2.8
  • Exemption tables 7.2.6.3.1, 7.2.6.3.2, 7.2.6.3.3, and 7.2.6.3.4
  • Enhancement spots to adjust SAP-delivered mixed-loading check rules.

Related Information

For more information about how to view the mixed loading check results, see Display of Dangerous Goods Data for Value Chain Processes.

For general information about the Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations (BADI_EHDGM_MIXED_LOAD) BAdI, see BAdI: Mixed Loading Check According to Dangerous Goods Regulations in the product assistance for Product Compliance. For more technical information, see the ABAP Element Info view for the BAdI in the ABAP Development Tools.

For more information about the mixed loading check, see Mixed Loading Check in the product assistance for Product Compliance.

For more information about developer extensibility, see Developer Extensibility in the product assistance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Remarks:

  • With this 2408 release, we only have mixed loading rules available for IMDG 2022
  • In the upcoming releases, we aim to introduce mixed loading rules for additional regulations.

 

Introduction of Segregation Codes for Products for the IMDG-Code Regulation

Related to the Mixed Loading Check Rules for IMDG 2022, the new Segregation Code for Products field is added to the “Classify Dangerous Good According to IMDG - Unpackaged Product” (F4514) app.

When you use this app to classify unpackaged products according to the IMDG-Code, you can now enter segregation codes for products that are classified as dangerous goods. You can select segregation codes that are delivered with the compliance requirement that represents this dangerous goods regulation.

 Picture 7: Segregation codes that are delivered with the compliance requirement.

For more information, see Classifying Unpackaged Dangerous Goods.

Where to Find More Information

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

For more information about Product Compliance, please see the Link Collection Blog.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series!

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a 50-minute-long recording and a presentation for Product Compliance as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

🔎 Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Where can I find learning material on sales solution processes?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sales Advanced Business Processes learning journey.

Overview
Within this Learning Journey, SAP application consultants can deepen their expertise of sales solution processes, specifically focusing on the most used advanced business processes.

This course is part of the series “Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sales”, which includes courses on the following subjects:

  1. Sales Fundamental Business Processes
  2. Sales Complaint Processing
  3. Sales Advanced Business Processes
  4. Sales Automation, Analytics and Configuration

Learning objectives
Consultants will achieve the following key learning objectives: 

  • Proficiency in Sales Processes: Master the execution and management of additional solution processes, including intercompany sales orders, sales using third-parties, rebate processing, down payments, collective billing, digital payments.

  • Practical Application: Develop hands-on experience through exercises in a real instance of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition. 

Prerequisites

  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sales Fundamental Business Processes

Book your certification exam
Validate your skills and learning by completing the certification exam and gain a highly recognised accreditation.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you.

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
This blog post provides an overview of innovations in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition release 2408 for the line of business Manufacturing.

Table of Contents

R&D/Engineering

  • Integration with CAD Design for Data and File Management (▶️demo video)

Production Engineering

  • Process Production Versions - Mass Edit (▶️demo video)
  • Production Routing API - Key User Extensibility

Production Planning

  • Manage Planned Orders - Mass Conversion
  • Capacity Scheduling Table – Extensibility (▶️demo video)
  • Drag and Drop in Capacity Scheduling Board (▶️demo video)
  • Monitor Material Coverage - Rescheduling Proposals (▶️demo video)

Production Operations

  • Manage Production Orders – Planning of Times (▶️demo video)

Quality Management

  • Integration of Advanced Variant Configuration (▶️demo video)
  • Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) – Download FMEAs as PDF files
  • Output Management for Defects
  • Manage Workflows for Quality Notification Task Processing

More Information / Additional links

 

For a crisp overview check out my video highlighting the following manufacturing release highlights:

  • Monitor Material Coverage - Rescheduling Proposals
  • Drag and Drop in Capacity Scheduling Board
  • Process Production Versions - Mass Edit
  • Manage Planned Orders - Mass Conversion
  • Integration of Advanced Variant Configuration and Quality Management

Video 1: Highlights for Manufacturing in the 2408 release

 

R&D/Engineering

Integration with CAD Design for Data and File Management

SAP PLM Product Data Integration (PDI), serves as a bridge that connects your CAD tools with SAP business processes. This integration is crucial in ensuring seamless data flow and enhancing the efficiency of your operations. A plugin extends the CAD to store files in PDI and derives an Enterprise Product Structure in SAP Product Lifecycle Management. Moreover, the product data can be transferred via Product Handover to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Watch the video below, in which my colleague Markus Oertelt shows the process in the system,

Video 2: Product Handover to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

If you are interested in more details about the PLM System Integration, Hand Over of Engineering BOM to Manufacturing BOM, and managing Change Records, check out the blog post from Markus that is planned to be published next week. The link to the planned blog post will be published here as well.

( 🔙 Table of Contents)

 

Production Engineering

Process Production Versions - Mass Edit

The Process Production Versions app enables production engineers now to perform mass edits for existing production versions. The new mass edit feature significantly reduces the time required for such tasks. You can make mass changes to the values in the editable fields of a production version, with the capacity to select up to 100 production versions for simultaneous updates. If the number of production versions selected exceeds 100, the count is displayed in red, next the Mass Edit button. This limitation is important for a good performance in foreground processing. It is planned to offer also background processing without limitation of selected production versions in a future release. And of course, the mass change capability includes validations while editing, ensuring accuracy and efficiency. 
In the video below I show you the new mass edit feature.

Video 3: Perform mass edits for existing production versions

( 🔙 Table of Contents)

Production Routing API - Key User Extensibility

With 2408, key user extensibility is supported for the ODATA API​ Production ​Routing (Version 2) [Technical name: API_PRODUCTION_ROUTING]. Users can Read, Create and Update custom fields​. Header, Operation, and Sub-operation entities are​ supported with this functionality​.

Figure 1: Read, Create and Update custom fields via Production Routing ODATA API

( 🔙 Table of Contents)

 

Production Planning

Manage Planned Orders - Mass Conversion

Figure 2: 'Manage Planned Orders' app - Mass Conversion

( 🔙 Table of Contents)

Capacity Scheduling Table – Extensibility

 

Video 4: 'Capacity Scheduling Table' app - Extensibility in the list-report for order master data and planned order header fields

( 🔙 Table of Contents)

Drag and Drop in Capacity Scheduling Board

With the past release, we enabled production planners to reschedule operations on the Manage Operation page of the Capacity Scheduling Board app by dragging and dropping the operation on the chart within the same work center (see my 2402 blog post).

Now, planners can reschedule an operation by simply dragging and dropping the operation across different production versions on the highlighted rows. - a feature designed to improve user experience and speed up the planning process. Read SAP Help chapter Reschedule Operations through Drag and Drop for a detailed description of the rescheduling capabilities in the Capacity Scheduling Board app.
Watch the video below in which Markus Oertelt shows you the new Drag & Drop capabilities.

Video 5: 'Capacity Scheduling Board' app - Drag and Drop

( 🔙 Table of Contents)

Monitor Material Coverage - Rescheduling Proposals

We've added additional columns in the Monitor Material Coverage apps, providing information on the number of rescheduling proposals for a material. This enables production planners to easily evaluate the planning situation and react swiftly to changes.
Markus Oertelt shows you the enhancements of the Monitor Material Coverage - Net Segments and Monitor Material Coverage - Net and Individual Segments apps in the next video.

Video 6: 'Monitor Material Coverage' app - Rescheduling Proposals

For detailed information, check out the following blog posts from Markus:​

  • Further evolution of MRP apps "Monitor Material Coverage"
  • Manage Supply Shortage and Excess Supply with MRP Material Coverage Apps

( 🔙 Table of Contents)

 

Production Operations

Manage Production Orders – Planning of Times

In the Manage Production Orders app, the Change Dates and Quantities popup has been enhanced with time
fields. They enable you to change planned start or planned end times for the scheduling types that offer the
planning of times, such as Forwards in Time and Backwards in Time.
Watch the video below in which I show you the new feature.

Video 7: 'Manage Production Orders' app – Planning of Times

( 🔙 Table of Contents)

 

Quality Management

Integration of Advanced Variant Configuration

With the 2408 release, Advanced Variant Configuration (aVC) is integrated in quality inspections with inspection lots.
The characteristics specified in the configuration of a sales order or purchase order are transferred to the
inspection characteristics of an inspection lot.
Watch the video below in which Markus Oertelt shows the process in the system.

Video 8: Integration of Advanced Variant Configuration and Quality Management

( 🔙 Table of Contents)

Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) – Download FMEAs as PDF files

If you’re asking yourself what an FMEA is, please check out the blog post

  • We might not know how to solve your problems, but we recommend using our FMEA app to prevent them

from Sonja Cornelißen.

For a short recap about the FMEA capabilities in Quality Management check out my blog posts FMEA launch with 2202 , FMEA enhancements with 2208 , FMEA enhancements with 2302, FMEA enhancements with 2308, and the FMEA expert talk.

With 2408, Quality Planners can download FMEAs as PDF files in the Manage FMEAs app. PDF files offer you the options of printing them, sending them as attachments, or storing them for documentation purposes. Each download is a snapshot of the current state of your FMEA, so with regular downloads, you can keep track of different versions of your FMEA.
The system uses a PDF-based form as the template for the PDF file. A predefined form is provided but you can also configure your own custom form for each FMEA type. In your configuration environment, a new optional configuration object is available: Assign PDF-Based Forms to FMEA Types. There you can specify the forms that are to be used for each FMEA type.

Figure 3: 'Manage FMEAs' app - Download FMEAs as PDF file

( 🔙 Table of Contents)

Output Management for Defects

Quality Engineers can use now SAP S/4HANA Output Control as the output management framework for defects. With this feature allows printing for all defect categories in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Figure 4: 'Manage Defects' app - Output Management

( 🔙 Table of Contents)

Manage Workflows for Quality Notification Tasks Processing

As a Configuration Expert - Business Process Configuration you can now configure a workflow for the processing of quality notification tasks. You configure the workflow with the Manage Workflows for Task Processing in QM app.

Figure 5: 'Manage Workflows for Task Processing in QM' app

Figure 6: 'Manage Workflows for Task Processing in QM' app - Workflow Details page

After configuration is done quality engineers will be notified about quality notification tasks in their 'My Inbox'.

Figure 7: My Inbox for Quality Engineer

( 🔙 Table of Contents)

 

More Information / Additional links

You will find a collection of SAP S/4HANA Cloud product centric blog posts from Markus Oertelt and me here:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Asset Management – The Link Collection
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Manufacturing & Supply Chain - The Link Collection
  • Discrete Industries in SAP S/4HANA Cloud - The Link Collection

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and @GerhardWelker.

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series!

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a 60-minute-long recording and a presentation for Manufacturing as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

🔎Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

Thanks for reading this blog post. Stay tuned!

( 🔙 Table of Contents)

entry.title: Environment, Health, and Safety in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
In the area of Environment, Health, and Safety (EHS), an essential practice known as Job Hazard Analysis (JHA) plays a pivotal role in safeguarding workers and promoting workplace well-being. JHA systematically examines job tasks to identify potential hazards, assess risks, and implement preventive measures.

Learn more about how the innovative Job Hazard Analysis in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition allows for the creation of a safer work environment.

Additionally, I will walk you through my selected key highlights for Environment, Health, and Safety:

To name just a few highlights:

  • New Application for Managing the Investigation Process,
  • Job Hazard Analysis,
  • User Notification for Reporting Tasks
  • Overview Page Providing Critical Insights of the Environmental Management Process.

In case you do not need all the information below and just briefly want to get an overview of the release highlights, please watch this video with the top two key innovations:

Release video: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 for Environment, Health, and Safety: Top 2 key innovations.

 

New Application for Managing the Investigation Process

Business Background

Investigations help in identifying the underlying causes of accidents or incidents, whether they are related to human factors, equipment failure, procedural errors, or environmental conditions. This thorough analysis is essential to develop effective corrective and preventive actions.

In the same way, properly managing investigations ensures compliance with legal requirements and regulatory standards. It demonstrates an organization's commitment to investigating incidents thoroughly and taking necessary actions to prevent similar incidents in the future.

Key Feature

This new 2408 release enables you to manage investigations of workplace incidents and document investigation results in a dedicated app. With the new “Manage Investigations” app (F7969), you can work on investigation records without needing authorization to edit the related incident records; you only need read access to the incidents.

You can plan the investigation process, set up an investigation team, collect evidence, analyze root causes, and make recommendations for ways to prevent similar incidents in the future.

 Demo 1: Empower expert users to document the investigation results without access to the incident.

For more information, see Manage Investigations.

Implementation Details

This feature will not automatically be available in your system but requires that you activate the new investigation process for the Incident Management (FTGL_IM_EHSPROD_6421) feature in the Activate Features configuration activity.

For more information, see Activate Features.

Note

After you activate the feature, you can only benefit from its full functionality if you also activate the overview page (OVP) floorplan for the “Edit Incident” app.

Integration between the new “Manage Investigations” app and the “Edit Incident” app is only available for the OVP floorplan.

You can activate the OVP floorplan using the Activate Overview Page Floorplan (New) configuration activity in your configuration environment.

For more information, see Configuration Environment of SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

 

Job Hazard Analysis

Job Hazard Analysis (JHA) is a method that focuses on analyzing job tasks to proactively identify hazards before they result in injuries. This approach highlights understanding the relationship between the worker, the task at hand, the tools and equipment being used, and implementing controls to reduce or eliminate potential hazards.

By sharing investigation findings, best practices, and lessons learned, organizations can promote a culture of continuous improvement and proactive risk management.

Key Feature

With this new 2408 release, we introduced a new SAP Fiori app “Manage Job Hazard Analyses”(F7896), With this innovative app, users can:

  • Perform job-based risk assessments and job hazard analyses to systematically evaluate each job step for potential hazards.
  • Identify job-related hazards and understand their implications on worker safety and workplace operations.
  • Implement controls and measures to treat identified hazards, ensuring a safer work environment for employees and reducing the likelihood of incidents or injuries.

 Demo 2: Define jobs and their job steps together with all of the hazards involved in each job step.

Configuration

To make a risk assessment type available in the “Manage Job Hazard Analyses” app, it must be defined and marked as Job-based in the corresponding configuration activity Configure Risk Assessment Types.

For more information, see Manage Job Hazard Analyses.

 

User Notification for Reporting Tasks

Business Background

The EHS in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition incorporates a dedicated Task Management solution that leverages SAP Workflow capabilities, enabling the creation of one-time and repetitive tasks with comprehensive functionality.

Tasks are delivered to users as workflow items accessible through the SAP Fiori “My Inbox app”.

Many users have EHS responsibilities that may not require SAP system access, making task management challenging. To address this, "alerting" events are now incorporated within tasks, allowing for automatic email notifications for task owners, assignees, and approvers.

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release introduces user notifications for real-time updates on task events, ensuring users stay informed of new tasks, assignments, and updates.

With the latest 2408 release, users can create and edit task definitions related to environmental reports, triggering the generation of reports on the task start date for user completion and approval.

Key Feature

With this new 2408 release, you can specify the events that trigger an automatic email notification sent to a task owner, assignee, or approver. You can set up the notifications for reporting tasks as desired so that the right person will receive an email as soon as a task fulfills a pre-specified condition (such as being completed, rejected, past due date, and so on).

Reporting tasks remain open until the environmental report is finalized and approved, streamlining the EHS task management process, and ensuring timely completion of environmental reports.

This makes it easier for the persons assigned to keep track of their tasks.

 Demo 3: Automatically inform involved users through e-mail about certain task statuses (task due, task overdue, task done, and so on).

Additional Details

There are predefined email templates for notifying the persons assigned to a task. The required information (for example, task title, task description, location, and due date) is filled in automatically by the system, and the email will be sent as soon as the task fulfills the previously set condition.

These templates are maintained by the system administrator in the “Maintain Email Templates” app. This app can also be used to create and adjust custom templates as a copy of the predelivery ones.

For more information, see Manage Reporting Tasks.

 

Overview Page Providing Critical Insights of the Environmental Management Process

Business Background

An Overview Page provides a consolidated view of key environmental performance indicators, regulatory requirements, and compliance status in a single interface. This streamlines monitoring processes, allowing for quick assessment of compliance metrics.

In the same way, centralizing environmental data and compliance information on a single platform enhances data visibility across the organization. This visibility enables stakeholders to access real-time information, track progress, and make informed decisions regarding compliance strategies.

Key Feature

This new 2408 release introduces an environmental dashboard to view all data issues with exceedances, warnings, and missing data collected within the Environment Management component from the last six months.

The “Environment Management Overview” dashboard contains five data viewing cards, which you can drag and drop in the order that you want to display the data on the screen. Three of the cards show a month-by-month graph of data issues that are found in the system from the last six months (counted back from the current date).

These numbers are collected for the location structure (including all sublocations) that the environmental manager is responsible for. This allows you, as the environmental manager, to access this data quickly and easily, all in one place.

For the cards displaying data:

  • Exceedances & Warnings (Approved Items)
  • Exceedances & Warnings (New Items - Action Required)
  • Missing Data

you can choose whether you want to display the collected data, calculated data, or aggregated data.

 Demo 4: Display critical environmental performance insights so managers can monitor and take proactive corrective actions.

 

Quick Links

This card contains navigation links to the following apps:

Manage Compliance Scenario - You can navigate here to view or edit the activities collecting data for your location, as well as assign or unassign environmental limits.

My Emissions Declarations

Monitor Data - Environment Management - You can navigate here to view and process the data issues shown in the overview.

Compliance Analysis - You can navigate here to view existing compliance requirement tasks.

Manage Compliance Requirements - Regulations, Permits, Policies - You can navigate here to view and edit compliance requirements and their environmental limits (which control when data exceedances and warnings are created).

 

Where to Find More Information

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

For more information about Environment, Health, and Safety, please see the Link Collection Blog.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 Early Release Series!

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Central Business Configuration 2406 FD2
entry.content:
With the latest feature delivery (FD2) for SAP Central Business Configuration, exciting new features are available. This blog post will provide you with a comprehensive overview of these features.

Support of Foreign Plants

It’s now possible to create a plant or shipping point in a country or region different from that of the company code, even when it may be outside your scope. As a prerequisite, you need to select scenario 5RP (Do you run business in a foreign country?) during scoping, and the foreign plant feature must be available for that country or region. This allows you to manage and operate supply chain processes abroad efficiently.

To learn more, check out this blog, in particular the Where RITA and the Foreign Plant Feature Can Be Used section.

Screenshot of a foreign plant in the Organizational Structure app

UX Enhancements for the Initiate Change Process

The Edit functionality has replaced the Initiate Change functionality and has been integrated into the respective apps. To make changes now, you simply choose Edit within the Scope, Organizational Structure, and Configuration apps. This update makes it more intuitive to make additions to previously completed activities.

Screenshot of the Edit dialog box

Harmonized Terminology

We've harmonized the terminology of cloud landscape information to enhance the user experience. This applies to both the Assign Deployment Target dialog box and the Deployment Target app. We’ve renamed the following elements:

  • Solution > Leading Product
  • Entitlement Role > System Role
  • Tenant URL > URL
  • Tenant ID > System Number

Furthermore, we’ve modified some activity names in the navigation pane for improved readability:

  • Define Scope > Scope
  • Business Processes Configuration > Configuration

Screenshot of the harmonized terminology in the Deployment Target app and the navigation pane

For a quick summary, check out this video about What's New in 2406, FD1 & FD2. Don’t forget to also use the What’s New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal, which provides further details and links about all the recent changes.

That wraps up the new features this time. Make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have an idea on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the SAP Customer Influence portal to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: Demystifying Peppol’s Message Types: Transport Acknowledgement, MLR, BLR
entry.content:
Are you exchanging electronic documents with your business partners through the Peppol network using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition? If so, you have probably come across terms such as Transport Acknowledgement (Acks), Message Level Response (MLR) or Business Level Response (BLR). And you might already know that all three refer to types of messages that are exchanged in Peppol's 4-corner model. However, it might not be completely clear to you when and why each of these message types is sent, what information they convey, or how they look in the eDocument Cockpit or the Manage Electronic Documents app of your SAP S/4HANA, SAP ERP, or SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. With this blog, we would like to bring some clarity into these questions.

Additionally, we will talk about message types developed by SAP to update the status of the electronic document in the SAP business system. Even though, these message types are not part of the OpenPeppol specification, we are mentioning them for the sake of completeness of all message types in the process of sending an electronic document using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.

 

Message Types

In general, you can think of the message types as a multi-stage check to ensure the reliable transmission of an electronic document over the Peppol network. Each message type confirms the status of a different stage in the transmission process.

Let's walk through the different message types using a concrete example. In the example, your business system is SAP S/4HANA Cloud, so the sender (corner 1) is represented by the Manage Electronic Documents app, and you are using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition (corner 2) to send electronic documents through the Peppol network to your business partners.

Example: Message types exchanged in Peppol’s 4-corner model when you send electronic documents

The starting point in the Peppol 4-corner model is the first corner where you, as the sender, submit an invoice in the Manage Electronic Documents app to your business partner. The document is then sent to the sender access point (corner 2) which is your SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition - this is the corner where the first message type is created.

Message type: Confirmation from SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition

Message Type: Confirmation from SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition
This message type is not part of the message types defined by OpenPeppol but was developed by SAP as an additional layer to ensure that your document has securely and correctly reached the sender access point.

In the cloud edition (your sender access point), two things are checked:

1. Has the document arrived at the sender access point?

  • If yes, the cloud edition sends a message to your business system and the status in your Manage Electronic Documents app changes to Received by Sender Access Point.
  • If no, the cloud edition does not send a message to your business system and the status in your Manage Electronic Documents app stays in Sending Requested.

2. Does the document pass the validation checks? The validation checks involve comparing the document with the Schematron rules stored in the sender access point and making sure the receiving participant in the Peppol network can be identified by the sender access point. If the document doesn’t pass the validation checks, the cloud edition sends a message to your business system and the status in the app changes to Error at Sender Access Point.

To resolve errors that appear at this stage you can check the Troubleshooting guide or the SAP Help Portal.

Message type: Transport Acknowledgement (Acks)

Message type: Transport Acknowledgement (Acks)

When your document has been sent to the sender access point and has successfully passed validation there, the sender access point (corner 2) forwards the document to the receiver access point (corner 3). The receiver access point creates the next message type – the transport acknowledgement.

The forwarding of the document to the receiver access point as well as the transport acknowledgement is accomplished using the Applicability Statement 4 (AS4) messaging protocol. It is based on web services standards and is commonly used for the secure and reliable exchange of documents such as purchase orders, invoices, and shipping notices.

The message transfer in AS4 involves the following steps:

  1. Message Creation: The sender access point creates the message to be sent, which typically includes the actual document and any necessary metadata.
  2. Message Packaging: The message is then packaged according to the AS4 standard, which defines the structure and format of the message. The packaging includes adding headers, security information, and other necessary elements.
  3. Adding Digital Signature: After the message has been packaged, a digital signature is generated using the sender's private key. This digital signature is added to the message. This not only validates the sender’s identity, but also ensures the integrity of the message during transmission as it can be used to detect any changes made to the message after it has been signed.
  4. Message Transmission: The packaged message along with the digital signature is transmitted over the Peppol network, using HTTP protocols.
  5. Message Receipt: Once the receiver access point receives the message, it verifies the digital signature using the sender’s public key. If the verification is successful, it indicates that the message was not tampered with during transmission and was indeed sent by the claimed sender.
  6. Transport Acknowledgment: After the successful verification of the digital signature, the receiver access point sends back an acknowledgment to the sender access point to confirm the successful receipt of the message.

Once the transport acknowledgement reaches the cloud edition (corner 2), the cloud edition sends a message to your business system (corner 1) that triggers the status update in the Manage Electronic Documents app to Acknowledged by Receiver Access Point.

In the case of an unsuccessful message transfer using the AS4 protocol, such as a header error in the message, the receiver access point can’t send a transport acknowledgment to the sender access point. However, it's important to note that the cloud edition (corner 2) still ensures transparency in this scenario by sending a message to your business system, triggering a status change in the Manage Electronic Documents app to Rejected by Receiver Access Point.

Overall, AS4 ensures secure and reliable message transfer in B2B communication, and the use of digital signatures adds an additional layer of security and trust to the process.

Message type: Message Level Response (MLR)

Message type: Message Level Response (MLR)

The receiver access point performs another round of validations on the invoice, particularly focusing on the Schematron rules. Since there are multiple versions of these rules, the sender access point and the receiver access point might be checking against different versions. Hence, a document might pass the validation at the sender access point and fail at the receiver access point.

The message that can be sent by the receiver access point to inform about the validation status is referred to as Message Level Response (MLR). Let’s have a look at the scenarios:

  • You have enabled receiving MLR in the Configure Participants app of the cloud edition.
    • If the receiver access point supports the sending of MLR, you receive an MLR message with the information whether the receiver access point accepted or rejected the electronic document after the validation. In your Manage Electronic Documents app you’ll therefore see either the status Accepted by Rec. Acc. Point or Valid. Error at Rec. Acc Point.
    • If the receiver access point doesn’t support the sending of MLR, you won’t receive an MLR message. In your Manage Electronic Documents app you’ll therefore see the status Acknowl. by Rec. Acc. Point.
  • You haven’t enabled receiving MLR in the Configure Participants app of the cloud edition: Then of course you won’t receive an MLR message and in your Manage Electronic Documents app you’ll see the status Acknowl. by Rec. Acc. Point.

MLR messages contribute to the overall robustness, reliability, and traceability of the Peppol network by enabling businesses to ensure that their business messages have been successfully received and processed, and quickly identify and rectify issues.

For more information about the Message Level Response, check out this page in the help portal: Message Level Response

Message type: Business Level Response (BLR)

Message type: Business Level Response (BLR)

Finally, the receiver access point forwards the document to the receiver (corner 4). The receiver system, or the users themselves, can then decide whether they wish to communicate something to the sender. This means, BLR messages are optional and may not be sent by the receiver. This message, originating from the receiver, is called a Business Level Response (BLR), as it provides information on the business decision relating to the business document.

Unlike the MLR, which is an automated technical confirmation of the message receipt and processing, the BLR concerns the actual outcome of the business message.

The BLR message is forwarded through all the corners and depending on the BLR the statuses in the Manage Electronic Documents app can change. For example, if the original document is an invoice, the following statuses can appear:

  • In Process by Receiver
  • Under Query by Receiver
  • Conditionally Accepted by Rec.
  • Acknowledged by Receiver
  • Accepted by Receiver
  • Rejected by Receiver
  • Partially Paid by Receiver
  • Paid by Receiver

For more information about the Business Level Response, check out this page in the help portal: Business Level Response.

How to check all those messages in your system

You can trace all the messages from the beginning to the end of the exchange process in the Manage Electronic Documents app in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system or in the eDocument Cockpit in your SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA system.

In the Manage Electronic Documents app, expand the Details of the electronic document.

Screenshot of the Manage Electronic Documents app with focus on the Details button

In the Attachments section, you can choose Preview to view the messages.

Screenshot of the Manage Electronic Documents app with focus on the Attachments section and the Preview button

In the eDocument Cockpit, navigate to Goto > History, to access the History of the electronic document.

Screenshot of the eDocument Cockpit with focus on the History button

In the EDocument History view, you can scroll to the right until you see the File Name column. By double-clicking on the files you can view the messages.

Screenshot of the EDocument History view in the eDocument Cockpit with the focus on the File Name column

How to enable the message types

If you are in the latest support package for your release, all message types that we previously described are already enabled in your business system for all countries supported by SAP that exchange invoices over the Peppol network. For the sending of Business Level Response, we currently only support Invoice Responses with Accepted and Rejected status, with the exception of Singapore, where we also support Invoice Responses with Acknowledged status.

In the cloud edition however, you must activate the Message Types in the Configure Participants app. This can be done while setting up a participant for your company, by selecting the various message types under the Additional Document Types field. See Registering Your Company to Send Electronic Documents in the Peppol Network.

If you are not in the latest support package of your SAP S/4HANA or SAP ERP system, you can manually implement the following SAP Note: 2783795 - Electronic Document Processing for Peppol Countries/Regions: Implementation Overview.

Further information

For further information about the message types that are part of the OpenPeppol specification, check this page: BIS Message Level Response 3.0

For detailed information about the subcategory Invoice Response of the Business Level Response type, check this page: BIS Invoice Response 3.2

Give us Feedback!

Please share your feedback and thoughts in a comment. If you have questions about SAP Document Compliance, you can post your question here.

entry.title: Artificial Intelligence & Technology in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408
entry.content:
Welcome to my blog post on Artificial Intelligence and Technologies, covering the latest features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Release 2408. In this release, we are excited to introduce a range of AI-driven functionalities that enhance user experience, improve operational efficiency, and support better decision-making. With features like Joule, AI-assisted financial insights, and intelligent resource matching, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Release 2408 is set to enhance your business operations, making them more intuitive, accurate, and efficient. Kudos to all our developers for their hard work and dedication.

 Watch my video to get a glimpse of the new features:

 

 

 

 

Joule

Joule is the new AI copilot in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, revolutionizing the way users interact with the system. It acts as your companion in navigating the complex SAP Ecosystem, offering transparent, role-based access to relevant processes from any location. With Joule, you experience a unified and delightful user interface across SAP's solution portfolio.

Joule facilitates conversational interactions, enabling seamless communication between humans and computers. It supports three primary conversational patterns:

  1. Navigational: Assisting users in locating the functionality they need within SAP systems.
  2. Transactional: Helping users efficiently complete various tasks.
  3. Informational: Retrieving relevant information from existing documents.

Leveraging generative AI, Joule provides many benefits, including:

  • Easy Navigation: Streamlined access to applications.
  • Fast Insights: Quick retrieval of business object information, such as purchase orders.
  • Seamless Access to Content: Summarized user enablement content for efficient task execution.

Moreover, Joule operates within various SAP BTP environments, including Cloud Foundry, Kyma, Kubernetes, or others. It is a multitenant application, ensuring data isolation and independent provisioning for different consumers.

Joule’s capabilities will be available in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in Continuous Delivery this year, offering users a more intuitive and productive experience.

 

 

AI-Assisted Financial Business Insights in Finance (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)

The "Virtual Analyst" within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition transforms financial data analysis by providing immediate explanations and summarizations of insights in natural language. This AI feature allows users to quickly discover what’s meaningful within selected data in Review Booklets, analyzing extensive data sets, highlighting dependencies, and proposing actions. Benefits include:

  • Immediate Insights: Quick explanations and summarizations of data insights in natural language.
  • Comprehensive Analysis: Analyzes extensive data sets, highlights dependencies, and proposes actions.
  • Intuitive Visualizations: Presents findings in visual, tabular, and plain language formats, aiding management reporting and decision-making.

Note: this feature is currently in Beta Testing Program. For more information, see in this link

 

 

AI-Assisted Configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions in Finance (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)

This feature leverages AI to automatically extract key information from natural language sentences or Excel files, setting up jurisdiction-based tax configurations with minimal manual input. The AI-assisted configuration for US Tax Jurisdictions reduces the effort required for users to configure tax settings accurately, minimizing manual errors. Benefits include:

  • Accuracy and Efficiency: Reduces manual errors and enhances setup accuracy.
  • Modernized Onboarding: Natural language interactions and an intuitive interface streamline the onboarding process, cutting down both time and costs.

Note: this feature is currently in Beta Testing Program. For more information, see in this link

 

AI-Assisted Error Resolution (SAP Advanced Financial Closing)

AI-Assisted Error Resolution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers a comprehensive solution to common error situations. It generates understandable explanations of technical error messages, providing end-users with detailed resolution steps. AI-Assisted Error Resolution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers:

  •   Comprehensible Explanations: Generates understandable explanations of technical error messages, making complex issues more accessible to end-users.
  •   Step-by-Step Guides: Provides detailed resolution steps to help users solve errors independently.
  •   Time Savings: Expedites error resolution during critical financial processes, reducing delays and operational pressure. This feature helps end-users resolve common errors independently, improving operational efficiency and reducing reliance on SAP support.

This feature significantly enhances the efficiency and effectiveness of financial reporting and decision-making processes.

Note: this feature is currently in Beta Testing Program. For more information, see in this link

 

 

AI-assisted Matching of Employees to Projects (SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Projects)

This advanced feature supports resource managers by intelligently matching skilled employees to project needs. It considers similar skills to those requested and maintained in the candidate profiles, displaying both the requested skills and the skills present in the profiles of matching candidates.

  •   Skill Matching: AI identifies candidates with the required or similar skills for project needs, simplifying the search process.
  •   Efficient Search: Resource managers can quickly find suitable employees for projects, ensuring optimal skill allocation and improved project outcomes.

This tool enhances the efficiency of project staffing and ensures that the right skills are allocated to the right projects, optimizing resource management processes.Note: this feature is currently in Beta Testing Program. For more information, see in this link.

 

 

AI-Assisted Smart Summarization (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)

With AI-Assisted Smart Summarization, users can obtain concise summaries of object pages based on SAP Fiori elements apps. This feature streamlines decision-making by providing clear and actionable information. Capabilities include:

  • Streamline Decision Making: Obtain concise summaries of object pages based on SAP Fiori elements apps.
  • Language Translation: Translate summaries into various languages.
  • Effortless Sharing: Use summaries as a foundation for follow-ups and share them easily across preferred platforms.

The summarizations can be used as a foundation for follow-ups and shared easily across preferred platforms, enhancing collaboration and communication.

This functionality is available for beta testing without additional licensing costs.

Note: this feature is currently in Beta Testing Program. For more information, see in this link

 

 

AI-Assisted Easy Filter (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition)

This feature enhances the filtering experience by generating results for inquiries in SAP Fiori elements-based list report applications using natural language. Users can verify and refine filter values through the classical filter experience or choose whatever filtering method suits their needs best. This feature enhances the filtering experience by:

  • Natural Language Queries: Generates results for inquiries in SAP Fiori elements-based list report applications using natural language.
  • Flexible Filtering: Allows verification and refinement of filter values through classical filter experiences.

The AI-Assisted Easy Filter makes it simpler and faster to find the information users need, improving the efficiency of data retrieval and analysis.

This functionality is available for beta testing without additional licensing costs.

Note: this feature is currently in Beta Testing Program. For more information, see in this link

 

Auto Enablement (Turnkey) of AI Use Cases

The Auto Enablement (Turnkey) feature simplifies the activation and management of AI use cases within SAP S/4HANA Cloud. It reduces or eliminates the manual effort required to initially activate and manage machine learning (ML) scenarios, ensuring that customers do not need extensive data science or process knowledge. This feature simplifies the activation and management of AI use cases by:

  • Reducing Manual Effort: Automates the initial activation and lifecycle management of ML scenarios.
  • No Expertise Required: Does not require data science or process knowledge from the customer.
  • Enhanced Model Quality: Improves business value by increasing model quality.

Users can easily activate and monitor AI use cases through a simple "Turnkey" switch.

 

 

Gen AI Use Cases Support via ISLM

The ISLM component in SAP S/4HANA Cloud enables seamless integration between applications and large language models (LLMs). It supports out-of-the-box generative AI use cases, facilitating their exploration, deployment, and activation. The ISLM component in SAP S/4HANA Cloud enables:

  • Seamless Integration: Acts as an AI integration component between applications and large language models (LLMs).
  • Out-of-Box Use Cases: Facilitates exploration and management of generative AI-based use cases.
  • Prompt Management: Provides basic prompt management for creating, generating, and extending prompt templates in business contexts.

These features empower users to leverage generative AI effectively within their business processes.

 

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. 

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

From July 8 to July 12, we hosted a series of 20 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a xx-minute-long recording and a presentation for LoB/Industry/Topic as well as 19 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. entry.title: Empowering SAP Partners - Service Partner User Management Application
entry.content:
SAP customers rely on high-quality implementation and operation services by SAP partners for their SAP cloud solutions. As an element of these services, SAP partners will often perform support-related activities and will engage with SAP support on behalf of their customers.

To empower these SAP partners, and to enable an expanded customer experience, SAP has introduced the service partner user management application, through which customers can authorize their partner’s S-user IDs to create and manage cases in SAP for Me on their behalf. This application is a key enabler for partner’s support related service quality, optimized troubleshooting, and collaboration between SAP and partners. In addition, it benefits SAP customers by resulting in accelerated troubleshooting of cases, higher transparency about partner behavior, and fewer S-user IDs to manage for partner users. SAP recommends that customers and partners leverage this application for any such scenarios where partners are engaging with SAP support at the request and on behalf of their customers. 

This blog post will share an overview of this application and the process and will be regularly updated when new features are added.

Process Overview

This application is natively hosted in SAP for Me. The process involves the use of the following cards in SAP for Me under the Users & Contacts dashboard:

  • Manage Service Partner Users: for use by customer’s cloud administrator, to invite and disengage partner’s S-user IDs, as well as manage the authorizations, installations, and validity periods
  • Service Partner User Cockpit: for use by partner S-users to accept invitations from customer as well as disengage the authorization

The process is completed as follows in SAP for Me:

1) Customer invites partner S-user(s) using Manage Service Partner Users (can be done one by one, or via CSV file upload)

2) Partner S-user accepts invitation in the Service Partner User Cockpit

3) Customer assigns the authorization to partner S-user, as well as specifying the installations and/or tenants they are authorized for and the authorization validity date

4) Partner S-user is now able to create and manage cases on behalf of customer within the Get Support application using their own S-user ID

New Features:

As of 6/1/2024 -

Customers now have the option to invite multiple S-users at a time using a CSV upload.

Restrictions:

  • CSV file only
  • Up to 200 entries

References

  • Customer Guide: https://d.dam.sap.com/a/sz1zkWg 
  • Customer KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/2518600

entry.title: Post Goods Receipt Without Reference in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Exploring Stock Procurement Processes: Goods Receipts Without Reference in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Discover how the "Post Goods Receipt Without Reference" app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud can streamline your procurement process by allowing you to post goods receipts without a purchasing document. This app is particularly useful for unplanned goods receipts that do not reference a preceding document.

Key Features of the Post Goods Receipt Without Reference App:

  • Post goods receipts without a purchasing document.
  • Enter details such as material number, quantity, unit, plant, storage location, and stock type.
  • Confirm entries and post the goods receipt.
  • Modify posting date and document date if needed.
  • Optionally, add notes and delivery notes.

App: Goods Receipt Without Reference app.

How to Use the App:

  1. Choose the Post Goods Receipt Without Reference app.
  2. Enter the required information:
    • Material number
    • Quantity / Unit
    • Plant
    • Storage Location
    • Stock Type
  3. Confirm your entry and choose Post.

In the General Information section, you can check and, if necessary, change the posting date and document date. You can also optionally enter a note and a delivery note (free text).

Visual Guide:

  • Before Posting:

  • After Posting: 

  • Generated Documentation:
  • Automated Flow in SAP Cloud:

Leverage the Post Goods Receipt Without Reference app to manage your unplanned goods receipts efficiently and maintain accurate stock levels.

entry.title: 使用8D问题解决方法的质量问题解决流程
entry.content:
面对质量缺陷问题，我们有很多工具可以用来分析和解决问题。其中一个比较典型的就是8D问题解决法（8 Discipline Problem Solving），也称团队导向问题解决法。在SAP公有云系统，我们也提供了基于8D问题解决法的问题解决流程。

当您在质量检验过程中发现了质量缺陷，可以在管理缺陷应用中针对某一个缺陷去启动问题解决流程：

这时，系统会自动为您创建一个问题解决流程的号码，点击该号码可以跳转至问题解决流程的详细界面中。

8D模型的问题解决流程，细分为8个工作步骤，包括组建团队、对缺陷问题的描述、根本原因的分析和阐述、后续更正/实施/预防的操作和措施等。

D1团队，目的是组建一个具备工艺和产品知识，具有分析和解决问题能力的小组，一般会有团队成员和团队领导。

点击问题解决流程中的D1团队，右上角的状态：设置为“处理中”，然后就可以添加小组成员了。

在D2问题描述中，可详细说明缺陷问题的情况，包括表象、数据等等。在D3包含操作定义为避免问题扩大或持续恶化而执行的临时遏制措施。

对于该缺陷问题，您需要分析出现问题的原因，并在D4根本原因中记录包括所有可能的潜在原因。在这里可以选择已定义好的原因代码，也可以添加详细的原因描述。

在D5定义的更正操作中您可以针对以上原因分别创建更正任务：

在质量任务中，您选择预定义好的任务代码，详细描写具体任务情况，指定任务处理人，和预计需要的工作时间：

完成定义更正操作中的任务后，系统会针对每一条任务生成一个实施更正操作的后继质量任务：

完成更正操作后，可定义相关的预防措施，如修改现有管理系统设置、更新设计图纸等：

最后在D8祝贺团队中对小组工作进行总结和祝贺。

至此，完成了整个质量问题的解决流程。

————————————————————————————————————————————
**本博文中涉及到的截图均来源于SAP内部测试环境。

entry.title: A Guide to Configure Maximum Proposals for Planned Orders & Fixed lot size of 1 for Production Order
entry.content:
A Guide for Setting Up Maximum Order Proposals for Planned Orders and Creating Production Orders with Fixed Lot Size One

Welcome to our blog!

In high-tech industries like semiconductors, tracking serial and batch numbers of BOM components is vital for effective after-sales service and quality management. One effective strategy to achieve this is by creating production orders with a fixed lot size of one. This blog post provides a comprehensive guide on how to set up and manage these production orders. By following the steps outlined, manufacturing teams can ensure accurate tracking of serial numbers and sub-level BOM components, ultimately enhancing overall production efficiency and traceability.

Business Requirement:

In high-tech industries like semiconductors, the manufacturing team aims to monitor the serial numbers of finished products and the sub-level BOM components used in their production. To achieve this, they maintain production orders with a fixed lot size of one and set the maximum order proposal to the highest possible limit (as the default limit for planned order proposals is 50, which might cause errors if the requirement quantity exceeds 50 at the time of MRP run). This method allows for precise traceability of lower-level BOM components and tracking of the serial numbers or batch numbers of components consumed in the manufacturing of the final finished product.

Below is a step-by-step process on how to set-up maximum order proposal for planned orders and to create production orders of fixed lot size ‘1’.

  • Maintain the maximum order proposal for Planned orders by accessing the following configurational path ‘Demand and Supply > Supply Chain Planning > Material Planning > Material Requirement Planning (MRP) > Define Plant Parameters Overview.’

         

  • Maintain the Number of planned orders to the maximum limit ‘999’ and save the configuration.

         

  • Maintain fixed lot size of ‘1’ in material master data using Fiori app Change Material (MM02).

         

  • Maintain Independent Requirement for the Material/Plant using Fiori app ‘Maintain PIRs.’ (In the below example, 5 quantities maintained for the period M08.2024).

         

  • Validate the Independent requirement maintained for that material/plant using Fiori app ‘Monitor Material Coverage – Net/Individual segments.

         

  • Perform MRP Run by clicking on the button ‘Start MRP Run’ in Fiori app Monitor Material Coverage.

         

  • Planned orders with fixed lot size ‘1’ has been created based on the requirement and these planned orders can be converted to production orders each with quantity 1EA using Fiori app Monitor material coverage or MD04 (Stock Requirement List).

         

  • Release the Production Order using Fiori app ‘Manage Production Orders’ or CO02 (Change Production Order)

         

  • Complete Goods Issue (Mvt. Type 261) of the BOM components using Fiori app ‘Post Goods Movement’.

         

  • Complete Goods Receipt (Mvt. Type 101) of the final product using Fiori app ‘Post Goods Receipt for Production Orders’.

         

Item Detail Screen:

         

  • Perform Confirmation of production order using Fiori app ‘Confirm Production Operation’.

         

  • Technically complete the production order using Fiori app CO02 (Change Production Order)
  • Once the order is complete, serial numbers issued and received in a production order can be tracked using a custom serial number tracker report. Please refer the SAP Community link to get the details on how to build a serial number tracker report for production materials in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Implementing a robust tracking system for serial numbers and BOM components in the semiconductor industry is crucial for maintaining product integrity and traceability. By following the step-by-step process outlined above, manufacturing teams can effectively set up maximum order proposals for planned orders and create production orders with a fixed lot size of one to a maximum limit of 999 orders. This meticulous approach ensures that every component used in the manufacturing process is accounted for, leading to improved quality control and customer satisfaction.

Hope this is useful and for any further queries, please post your queries in the SAP Community Portal

Happy Learning!!
